rulings.Case Title: IBRAHIM KAILEH ET AL VS. SAN FRANCISCO RESEDENTIAL RENT STABILIZATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Hearing Of Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 11, 2019 line 1. PETITIONERS IBRAHIM KAILEH, MAHA KAILEH'S Re-Notice Of Hearing Of Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate CONTINUED to February 12, 2019 at 9:30am in Dept. 501 on Court's own motion. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: AMY SIU PING CHAN ET AL VS. JIM K FUNG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Approval Of Proposed Distribution Of Proceeds Of Sale, For Authority To Make Distribution And Request To Be Discharged As Referee
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 11, 2019 line 2. OTHER AMY HARRINGTON'S Motion For Approval Of Proposed Distribution Of Proceeds Of Sale, For Authority To Make Distribution And Request To Be Discharged As Referee GRANTED as follows: 1. Notice of hearing of this report and motion was given as required by law; 2. This Court approves all prior disbursements; 3. Amy Harrington's referee fees and costs are approved in the amount of $48,301.00, which includes feees and costs in the cum of $39,707.36, previously approved by this Court on October 5, 2018; 4. Counsel Gordon Yamamoto attorney fees and costs are approved in the amount of $30,265.37, which includes fees and costs in the sum of $28,167.37 previously approved by this Court on October 5, 2018; 5. Counsel Warren Woo's attorney fees and costs are approved in the amount of $54,639.03; 6. Distribution to Co-Trustees Amy Chan and Cindy Fung approved in the amount of $469,546.24; 7. Distribution to Linda Fung approved in the amount of $234,773.11; 8. Distribution to Jin Q. Fung approved in the amount of $47,434.00; 9. This Court approves $25,000 of the sales proceeds to be held in reserve for a period of up to ninety (90) days for additional expenses; 5. This Court authorizes Gordon Yamamoto to be paid for up to 5 hours of additional attorney tune and costs without further order of this Court; 6. This Court authorizes Amy Harrington to be paid for up to 10 hours of additional referee time and costs without further order of this court; 7. This Court discharges Amy Harrington as Referee in this matter upon proof of final disbursement of the closing reserve filed with the Court. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: RUSSELL S. ZAPPETINI ET AL VS. JOHN MCCAIN KIM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 11, 2019 line 3. DEFENDANT MAN KIM'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is CONTINUED to January 28, 2019. Both parties are ordered to comply with SFLR 2.7B and provide courtesy copies of moving papers and opposition to Department 501 no later than January 22, 2019, with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: GRUBER & GRUBER PROPERTIES VS. MARCUS ROSNIK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Application For Order To Enter Judgment Pursuant To Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 11, 2019 line 5. Application For Order To Enter Judgment Pursuant To Settlement Agreement DENIED. By not posting written warning on Defendant's door after one alleged incident, Plaintiff failed to comply with page 3 lines 9-10 of the operative Settlement Agreement with Stipulation for Entry of Future Judgement. Pursuant to the stipulation, Plaintiff was not permitted to seek ex parte enforcement to terminate Mr. Rosnik's tenancy. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN BRITTON ET AL VS. JOSE JUAN RAMIREZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 11, 2019 line 6. DEFENDANTS JOSE RAMIREZ, VIVIANA RODRIGUEZ'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. Moving party shifted its burden. Opposition failed to create a triable issue of fact. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. PATRICIA HARRIS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 11, 2019 line 8. PLAINTIFF THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend to allege in good faith allege facts sufficient to sustain constitute an affirmative defense as to: (1) Breach if Warranty of Habitability, (2) Plaintiff Waived, Changed, or Canceled Notice, (3) Estoppel by Conduct, (4) Doctrine of Laches. = (501/CFH)
